title: Detection of alpha-synuclein aggregates in floating sections using antibody 5G4
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5lrmp","text":" 5G4 is an antibody with an epitope in the region of aa44-57 of human alpha-synuclein protein, with a high specificity for aggregates. 5G4 reacts with a wide variety of disease aggregates from oligomers to fibrillar type, and shows very little reactivity for physiologic monomeric asyn, making it an excellent generalized “marker” for misfolded pathogenic asyn species. Use of this antibody has been limited to paraffin-embedded sections. Here, we developed a working protocol for fixed floating 40-micron human brain sections. Using the thicker tissue format may help apply 5G4 to a wide variety of specimens, including thicker peripheral tissue biopsies, which are the preferred format for use as a biomarker.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"9kesg","text":"Mount tissue on slide:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":22}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fprk7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1rtc1","text":"Mount tissue on superfrost plus adhesion slides and dry overnight in oven at 37 (make sure it’s very dry).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"56ggp","text":"5G4: Anitbody can be purchased from millipore-sigma (Millipore Cat# MABN389, RRID:AB_2716647)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f2i1u","text":"  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"e29v","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"id":968163,"guid":"891725EB502D11F0A7660A58A9FEAC02","name":" Anti-α-Synuclein (SNCA) Antibody","mol_weight":0,"sku":"MABN389","url":"https://www.sigmaaldrich.com/IN/en/product/mm/mabn389","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":17,"name":"Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich)","link":"http://sigmaaldrich.com","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: Di water for 00:02:00 .
Xylenes 2x10 min in the chemical hood.
100% ethanol 2x5 min each in a chemical hood. (100% ethanol is denature) (absolute ethanol is the anhydrous.
95% ethanol for 00:03:00 .
70% ethanol for 00:03:00 .
50% ethanol for 00:03:00 .
Xylenes 2x10 min in the chemical hood.
70% ethanol for 00:03:00 .
95% ethanol for 00:03:00 .
100% ethanol 2x5 min each in a chemical hood. (100% ethanol is denature) (absolute ethanol is the anhydrous.
Di water for 00:02:00 .
50% ethanol for 00:03:00 .
Wash sections with sodium citrate buffer for 00:05:00 .
Let citrate acid to cool ~ 00:30:00 .
Microwaving: 00:10:00 in citrate buffer (pH 6). 1000 L H 2 O 2.94 g sodium citrate (dihydrate) PH to 6.0 0.5 mL Tween -20
Quick wash with DM. DM preparation: A B 1 Tris-HCl 50mM 2 NaCl 150mM 3 Triton X-100 0.05%
00:30:00 in 88% formic acid.

Make blocking buffer: DM + 3% BSA + 3% serum+ Triton. For 100 mL: A B 1 DM 100 mL 2 BSA 3 g 3 Serum 3 mL 4 Triton 0.5 mL 2. Serum must be from host species of secondary antibody 3. Take 50 mL out for blocking buffer and leave the other 50 for primary antibody 4. Add peroxidase quenching to the blocking buffer solution For 50ml: A B 1 30%H 2 O 2 0.5 mL 2 Sodium azide (stock 10%) 0.5 mL
Dip in TBS 2x5 min each. This removes excess DM which interferes with the hydrophobic barrier.
Dip slides in blocking buffer for 01:30:00 at Room temperature .
5G4 should be diluted 1:5000.
Cover the tissue section in primary antibody solution. Overnight at 4 °C .
Dip in DM 3x5 min.
Cover sections in the secondary antibody for 01:00:00 at Room temperature .
Dilute secondary antibody (Horse Anti-Mouse IgG Antibody (H+L), Biotinylated (BA-2000-1.5) from VectorLabs) in secondary blocking buffer (for biotinylated secondary 1:200).
Make secondary blocking buffer: For 50 mL working solution: A B 1 DM 50 mL 2 BSA 1 g 3 Serum 2 mL
Dip in TBS 2x5 min.
Dip in TBS 2x5 min.
Dip in DM 3x5 min.
Prepare ABC reagent. Must be prepared 00:30:00 prior to use. Stable for 24:00:00 after prep. For 25mL: 2.5 mL blocking buffer + 1 drop A + 1 drop B After 30 minutes, fill to 25 mL
Dip in DM 00:05:00 .
Cover sections with prepared ABC reagent for 01:15:00 at Room temperature .

Make imidazole buffer (IB). For 1 L : 6.8 sodium acetate + 0.68 imidazole. Once IB is homogeneous, separate 100 mL into a separate beaker with stirbar. For 900 mL of IB, pH to 7.2-7.4. For 100 mL of non-pH’d IB, add 50 mg DAB followed by 2.5 g nickel. Dissolve completely.
Once done developing wash sections in PBS 2x10min.

Monitor color development. (Nickel DAB = jet black, DAB = brown). To check color development slides can be placed in a coplin jar with pH’d IB and then checked under microscope. If color intensity is too weak, place sections back into DAB solution for additional time. Can repeat this procedure until desired color intensity is achieved.

Dip sections in DAB solution for development. Strong signals 00:00:30 - 00:02:00 Medium signals, 00:02:00 - 00:05:00 Weaker signals 00:05:00 - 00:10:00 . Development up to 10 min is typically free of background. Over 10 min and background may develop.
Immediately before use add hydrogen peroxide to DAB solution. For 100mL DAB solution add 16 µL of 30% H 2 0 2 .
Dip slides in pH’d IB (i.e. pH 7.2-7.4) 3x10 min each.
Deactivate DAB solution with bleach and dispose in DAB waste container.
100% ethanol 00:05:00 each in a chemical hood. (100% ethanol is denature) (absolute ethanol is the anhydrous. (1/2)
100% ethanol 00:05:00 each in a chemical hood. (100% ethanol is denature) (absolute ethanol is the anhydrous. (2/2)
Xylenes 00:10:00 in the chemical hood. (1/2)
Xylenes 00:10:00 in the chemical hood. (2/2)
Xylenes 00:10:00 in the chemical hood. (1/2)
Xylenes 00:10:00 in the chemical hood. (2/2)
100% ethanol 00:05:00 each in a chemical hood. (100% ethanol is denature) (absolute ethanol is the anhydrous. (1/2)
100% ethanol 00:05:00 each in a chemical hood. (100% ethanol is denature) (absolute ethanol is the anhydrous. (2/2)
Dip in TBS 00:05:00 each. (1/2)
Dip in TBS 00:05:00 each. (2/2)
Dip slides in pH’d IB (i.e. pH 7.2-7.4) 00:10:00 each. (1/3)
Dip slides in pH’d IB (i.e. pH 7.2-7.4) 00:10:00 each. (2/3)
Dip slides in pH’d IB (i.e. pH 7.2-7.4) 00:10:00 each. (3/3)
Once done developing wash sections in PBS 00:10:00 . (1/2)
Once done developing wash sections in PBS 00:10:00 . (2/2)
